incidents.name: incident_number
incidents.comment: Unique identifier per incident.

incidents.name: line_number
incidents.comment: Numeric codes here mapped to as colors

incidents.name: primary_cause
incidents.comment: Blank by design for station incidents (symptom is the cause_. Also blank for train incidents whose analysis has not landed yet (can take 30+ days). Those two blanks mean different things

incidents.name: secondary_cause
incidents.comment: Blank by design for station incidents

incidents.name: symptom
incidents.comment: Initial cause assigned at the moment of the call

incidents.name: route
incidents.comment: Route number of the train involved. Zero-padded string.

incidents.name: vehicle
incidents.comment: Vehicle number of the train involved. String not int

incidents.name: car_door
incidents.comment: Values 1-8 or 'toutes'. String, not int 'all' is a legit value.

incidents.name: hardware_type
incidents.comment: Rolling stock type

incidents.name: cat
incidents.comment: Traction power cut indicator

incidents.name: material_damage
incidents.comment: Possible damage to the train indicator

incidents.name: kfs
incidents.comment: Customer pulled the emergency brake indicator

incidents.name: door
incidents.comment: A door was involved in the incident.

incidents.name: emergency_metro
incidents.comment: Metro emergency team dispatched at the control room's request

incidents.name: evacuation
incidents.comment: Free text describing what was evacuated (car / train / station / station and train). Not enumerated in the dictionary

incidents.name: location_code
incidents.comment: Location of the incident

incidents.name: time_of_incident
incidents.comment: For trains this is the start of the customer-facing delay, NOT the start of the incident

incidents.name: resumption_time
incidents.comment: For trains this is the end of the customer-facing delay

incidents.name: incident_in_minutes
incidents.comment: Present in the CSV but absent from the published dictionary. Verify against resumption_time minus time_of_incident before trusting it

incidents.name: incident_date
incidents.comment: Civil day of the incident. Not in your original column map - drop this block if the column is absent from your extract.

incidents.name: calendar_year
incidents.comment: Coverage starts 2019-01-01.

incidents.name: calendar_year_month
incidents.comment: as titled

incidents.name: calendar_day
incidents.comment: as titled

incidents.name: day_of_the_week
incidents.comment: as titled

mileage.name: line_number
mileage.comment: Dictionary says only 'name of the line' without enumerating values - these are the expected colour names. If the file uses numeric codes instead, the values pass through untouched and you should copy the incidents block's map here instead.

mileage.name: route
mileage.comment: Documented domain is 001, 050, 060, 066, 999. Zero-padded string, not int, or 001 becomes 1 and stops matching the incidents file. Both header spellings resolve to this one block.

mileage.name: operating_period
mileage.comment: Five list periods per year. The CSV may use codes or abbreviations rather than full month names - check before relying on the map.

mileage.name: day_type
mileage.comment: Documented as 'code for the type of weekday' with no value list published. Enumerate from the CSV and extend this block - it matters, because weekday/weekend service levels differ enough to distort any un-normalised comparison.

mileage.name: planned_mileage
mileage.comment: Planned, not actual. A major interruption cuts actual km while planned km stays flat, which deflates any incident-per-km rate exactly when reliability is worst. State this as a limitation wherever you publish a normalised rate.

mileage.name: calendar_year/week
mileage.comment: No equivalent column in the incidents file - derive one there if you want weekly normalisation.

mileage.name: calendar_day
mileage.comment: The finest-grain join key available against the incidents file.

mileage.name: day_of_the_week
mileage.comment: Enumerated explicitly in the published dictionary for this dataset.
